config.seed_text: The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.reflection_text: In this case, we read "I'm going to make it so there is no writing" instead of the written word ("We shall go back further"), because Locus was not built for poets; he said at any rate his grammar had improved significantly since then (and perhaps some time later) due largely from reading about other peoples' history... as well I guess - when you get used only by yourself or just looking with eyes). Anyway… my favorite part here? The fact that each note has one idea followed by another which leads us directly onto something important like why an enemy lost half their territory – i.. er, they got all your warring states together anyway! It took me quite awhile before everything turned into 3 pieces until finally starting up again :-( ) And now maybe every once in

Just wondering who started talking around these topics early?! Also see http://sparksblogger741_slideshare.blogspot.(pdf), https:/ *(https:\/\/webbbs2l6t5g1nx3vqjmbfd0pzcwijld4uql9rkptyh8i= ). reflection_generation.prompt:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.
2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.
reflection_generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.
2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions. In this case, we read "I'm going to make it so there is no writing" instead of the written word ("We shall go back further"), because Locus was not built for poets; he said at any rate his grammar had improved significantly since then (and perhaps some time later) due largely from reading about other peoples' history... as well I guess - when you get used only by yourself or just looking with eyes). Anyway… my favorite part here? The fact that each note has one idea followed by another which leads us directly onto something important like why an enemy lost half their territory – i.. er, they got all your warring states together anyway! It took me quite awhile before everything turned into 3 pieces until finally starting up again :-( ) And now maybe every once in

Anyway… my favorite part here? rewrite_generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Use the reflection below to write the next evolved version of the text. Keep it as connected prose, not bullet points.

ORIGINAL TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.

REFLECTION:
In this case, we read "I'm going to make it so there is no writing" instead of the written word ("We shall go back further"), because Locus was not built for poets; he said at any rate his grammar had improved significantly since then (and perhaps some time later) due largely from reading about other peoples' history... as well I guess - when you get used only by yourself or just looking with eyes). Anyway… my favorite part here? The fact that each note has one idea followed by another which leads us directly onto something important like why an enemy lost half their territory – i.. er, they got all your warring states together anyway! It took me quite awhile before everything turned into 3 pieces until finally starting up again :-( ) And now maybe every once in a while someone can start working out exactly how such things work / what could happen if human life does actually exist/then our new species gets ready to conquer them after years under occupation :) So far everyone seems to agree though -- but please don't worry.... most people seem really happy ;) This thing makes sense given both economics knowledge & practical experience . Just wondering who started talking around these topics early?! Also see http://sparksblogger741_slideshare.blogspot.(pdf), https:/ *(https:\/\/webbbs2l6t5g1nx3vqjmbfd0pzcwijld4uql9rkptyh8i= ). Don` t forget also 'mature'. There might be better ways ...

Just wondering who started talking around these topics early?! Also see http://sparksblogger741_slideshare.blogspot.(pdf), https:/ *(https:\/\/webbbs2l6t5g1nx3vqjmbfd0pzcwijld4uql9rkptyh8i= ). Don` t forget also 'mature'. There might be better ways ...
